revision.text: #redirect[[en:Schetui]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N37:G4-X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t''
lies: ''Št.wj'' }} '''Schetui''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Gemeinsam bildete Schetui mit dem Dekan [[Ab (Dekan)|Ab]] das altägyptische [[Sternbild]] [[Ab-Schetui]]. Eine Zuordnung der auffälligsten [[Stern]]e gestaltet sich schwierig. Als Sternbilder kommen [[Wasserschlange (Sternbild)|Wasserschlange]] und [[Löwe (Sternbild)|Löwe]] in Frage. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Schetui am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 36. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|26. Peret IV]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleres Reich]] |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (lies: ''Št.wj'' )
''Schildkröte'' |BILD1 = Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Gemeinsam bildete Schetui mit dem Dekan [[Ab (Dekan)|Ab]] das altägyptische [[Sternbild]] [[Ab-Schetui]]. Der Dekan Schetui ist mit dem ersten Teil des Sternbildes [[Krebs (Sternbild)|Krebs]] gleichzusetzen, dessen [[Stern]]e [[Krebs (Sternbild)# Himmelsobjekte|ι-Cancri]], [[Asellus Borealis]] und [[Asellus Australis]] den Kopf der [[Schildkröte]] bildeten. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Schetui am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 36. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|26. Peret IV]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950, S. 12–13. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950, S. 12–13. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleres Reich]] |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[Neues Reich]] |NAME2 = N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (lies: ''Št.wj'' )
''Schildkröten'' |BILD1 = Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Gemeinsam bildete Schetui mit dem Dekan [[Ab (Dekan)|Ab]] das altägyptische [[Sternbild]] [[Ab-Schetui]]. Der Dekan Schetui ist mit dem ersten Teil des Sternbildes [[Krebs (Sternbild)|Krebs]] gleichzusetzen, dessen [[Stern]]e [[Krebs (Sternbild)# Himmelsobjekte|ι-Cancri]], [[Asellus Borealis]] und [[Asellus Australis]] den Kopf der [[Schildkröte]] bildeten. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Schetui am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 36. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|26. Peret IV]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. In der Regierungsdauer von [[Ramses II.]] war die [[Akronychisch|akronychische]] [[Kulmination (Astronomie)|Kulmination]] von Schetui für den [[Achet#Datierungen|vierten Achetmonat]] angesetzt, woraus sich ein möglicher Zeitraum für 1279 v. Chr. vom 25. September bis 24. Oktober und für 1214 v. Chr. vom 12. September bis 11. Oktober ergibt. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950, S. 12–13. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

''Schildkröten'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Gemeinsam bildete Schetui mit dem Dekan [[Ab (Dekan)|Ab]] das altägyptische [[Sternbild]] [[Ab-Schetui]]. Der Dekan Schetui ist mit dem ersten Teil des Sternbildes [[Krebs (Sternbild)|Krebs]] gleichzusetzen, dessen [[Stern]]e [[Krebs (Sternbild)# Himmelsobjekte|ι-Cancri]], [[Asellus Borealis]] und [[Asellus Australis]] den Kopf der [[Schildkröte]] bildeten. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Schetui am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 36. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|26. Peret IV]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. In der Regierungsdauer von [[Ramses II.]] war die [[Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|akronychische Kulmination]] von Schetui für den [[Achet#Datierungen|vierten Achetmonat]] vermerkt, woraus sich ein möglicher Zeitraum für 1279 v. Chr. vom 25. September bis 24. Oktober und für 1214 v. Chr. vom 12. September bis 11. Oktober ergibt. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950, S. 12–13. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Schetui]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. ''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (Decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (Decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (Decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October qq). == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. ''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (Decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]][[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]][[sn:chetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]][[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schetui]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]][[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schetui]][[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[Category:11]][[sn:chetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]]

''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|12=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|12=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|12=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|12=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|12=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|12=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]]

The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|12=draft|12=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|12=draft|12=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|12=draft|12=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|12=draft|12=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]]

The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|qq=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]]

During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. ''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|qq=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Schetui]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|qq=draft}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Schetui]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|de|Neues Reich|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decade (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan|qq=}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|qq=}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constellation|qq=}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|qq=}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cancer (constellation)|qq=}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star|qq=}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iota Cancri|qq=}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamma Cancri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Cancri|qq=}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesses II|qq=}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akronychical culmination|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Schetui]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[sn: failed]]

Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|qq=}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constellation|qq=}} {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|qq=}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|de|Krebs (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cancer (constellation)|qq=}} constellation, where the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star|qq=}}s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iota Cancri|qq=}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamma Cancri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Cancri|qq=}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of {{ill|Ramesses II|de|Ramses II.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesses II|qq=}}, the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akronychical culmination|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Schetui]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME = I2-Z2:X1 N14-N33*N33:N33 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME2 = N14-N37:V13-G43-I2-I2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Schetui
''Štw.t'' (read: ''Št.wj'')
''Turtles'' |BILD1 = Sternbild Krebs.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 280px }} '''Schetui''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). Together with the decan {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab (Dekan)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|qq=}}, Schetui formed the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|qq=}}. The decan Schetui corresponds to the first part of the [[:en:Cancer (constellation)|Cancer (constellation)]] constellation, where the [[:en:Star|Star]]s {{ill|Iota Cancri|de|ι Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iota Cancri|qq=}}, {{ill|Gamma Cancri|de|Gamma Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamma Cancri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Delta Cancri|de|Delta Cancri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Delta Cancri|qq=}} formed the head of the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Schetui represented the 36th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=26th Peret IV|de|26. Peret IV|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. During the reign of [[:en:Ramesses II|Ramesses II]], the {{ill|Akronychical culmination|lt=akronychische Kulmination|de|Akronychisch#Akronychische Kulmination sowie akronychische Auf- und Untergänge bestimmter Sterne|1=Acronychic|lt=Acronychical culmination|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akronychical culmination|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} of Schetui was recorded for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=fourth Achet month|de|vierten Achetmonat|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, indicating possible periods for 1279 BCE (September 25 to October 24) and 1214 BCE (September 12 to October 11). == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schetui]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schetui]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schetui]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Schetui]] [[ja:シェトゥイ]] [[en:draft:Schetui]] [[de:Schetui]] [[sn:chetui]] [[sn: failed]]
